# What it all means {#sec-meaning}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Scaffold — an outline of intended content and its source artifacts, not finished prose.
:::

*[Scaffold]* The synthesis. Separates what we can claim from what we can only hypothesise.

## The causal core, restated {#sec-meaning-core}

*[Scaffold]* The randomised findings (@sec-itt, @sec-joint) and their robustness (@sec-robustness), in plain terms with their uncertainty.

## The associational ring, as hypotheses {#sec-meaning-associations}

*[Scaffold]* The mechanism / mediation / alternative-view results re-filed explicitly as **hypotheses to test**, not findings — the DAG forbids reading them as effects (@sec-dag-traps).

## Practical reading {#sec-meaning-practical}

*[Scaffold]* What the robust results imply for teaching and support — the thread the separate plain-language publication will expand.
